pr.title: PR #125 — Capability Summary Framework v1
pr.description:
## Summary

Adds the **Capability Summary Framework v1** — the first *understanding* layer on top of the Global Capability Registry (#122, identity) and the Capability Event Timeline (#123, memory). Where the registry says *what* a capability is and the timeline says *what happened*, this layer says *what it means*: a human-readable purpose/summary/architectural-significance plus a deeper narrative (context, rationale, risks, lessons, future opportunities). Summaries and narratives are append-only and ordered deterministically; raw payloads and schema versions are preserved verbatim.

> Note: the spec was titled "#124" but that number is the already-merged GitHub Metadata Import PR (#13), so this is **#125**. It consumes only the registry + timeline layers (both already on `main`) and does not depend on #124.

```
create(summaries, narratives, raw_metadata):
    summaries  = [CapabilitySummary.from_dict(s) for s in summaries]   # require capability_id + capability_name
    narratives = [CapabilityNarrative.from_dict(n) for n in narratives] # require capability_id
    summaries.sort(key=(created_at, capability_id, capability_name))    # deterministic
    narratives.sort(key=(created_at, capability_id, narrative_id))      # deterministic
    capability_counts = sorted {capability_id: #summaries+#narratives}
    persist report.json + evidence bundle
append(report_id, ...): existing items preserved verbatim, new items added, whole set re-sorted (append-only)
```

**Pass/fail:** `passed = (summary_count > 0)` — a report with at least one summary carries understanding; an empty report fails.

## What changed

- `src/axiom_core/capability_summary.py` (new): models `CapabilitySummary` / `CapabilityNarrative` / `CapabilitySummaryReport` (with `capability_counts`) / `CapabilitySummaryEvidence` + `CapabilitySummaryEngine`. Path-sandboxed evidence under `artifacts/capability_summary/<run_id>/`; JSON/Markdown/CSV export. Engine exposes `create` / `append` (append-only) / `get_report` / `list_reports` / `export_report`.
- `src/axiom_cli/main.py`: `axiom capability-summary-create` (`--summary-file`, `--json-output`), `capability-summary-show`, `capability-summary-export` (`--format markdown|json|csv`). Generates `capability_summary.md` + `capability_narrative.md` as dedicated understanding documents within the evidence bundle.
- Registered in `command_registry.py`, `test_selection_engine.py`, `tests/test_command_registry.py`.
- `tests/test_capability_summary.py` (new): models round-trip, deterministic ordering, capability counts, raw payload + schema preservation, append-only behavior, exports, evidence bundle, pass/fail, path safety.

## What behavior changed
New additive CLI commands + module. No existing runtime behavior changed.

## What did NOT change
No architecture changes, no broad refactoring, no new dependencies, no network calls, no GitHub API, no metadata ingestion, no orchestration/routing/planning/repair/graph-engine. Existing registry/timeline artifacts are never mutated. Revit 2024 baseline unaffected.

## Tests run
- `poetry run pytest -q` → **4061 passed, 1 skipped** (+38; baseline 4023 at #124).
- `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` → **0 errors**.
- CLI smoke: create (3 summaries / 2 narratives, deterministic ordering by `(created_at, capability_id)`, sorted `capability_counts`), show round-trip, all 3 export formats, `show missing` → exit 2, `export ../../etc` → path-safety exit 1.

## Validation still pending
Optional recorded CLI walkthrough (adversarial out-of-order fixture) on request.

## Known risks
Low. Self-contained new module + additive CLI commands; no changes to existing runtime behavior. No Revit live validation required; 2024 baseline unaffected.

## Strategic fit
Extends the identity → memory → understanding progression: registry (identity) + timeline (memory) + summary (understanding) form the first three pillars of a future organizational graph. Evidence bundles feed the registry/promotion direction rather than remaining isolated run outputs.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
